title: members: portal: no `[components.portal]` provider abstraction in nectar.toml
body:
Ghost's Portal (`portal.min.js`) is loaded automatically by themes via `data-portal="signup|signin|account|upgrade|recommendations"` triggers. Nectar has no server and no built-in portal. Themes that ship the example/Source theme reference `data-portal` 6× in `navigation.hbs` and `post-list.hbs` — those buttons are dead in a Nectar build.

**What Ghost provides**: a single JS bundle that injects an iframe modal handling signup, signin (magic link), account page, paid upgrades, and recommendations.

**What themes expect**: clicking `<button data-portal="signup">` opens a signup modal; the form posts to `/members/api/...` Ghost endpoints.

**What Nectar should provide**: a config-driven adapter so themes keep working unchanged.
```toml
[components.portal]
provider = "buttondown" # or beehiiv | substack | convertkit | bentonow | mailerlite | custom
publication = "my-newsletter"
signin_url = "https://buttondown.email/login"
account_url = "https://buttondown.email/account"
```
Impl plan: register a build-time post-render transform that scans for `data-portal="…"` and rewrites `href`/`onclick` to the provider's URLs. Provide a tiny `nectar-portal.js` shim that intercepts clicks and opens the provider in a popup. Document the gap (no `@member`, no per-user state) — the shim is one-way (signup only).

title: members: portal: `<button data-portal="signup">` renders but does nothing without runtime
body:
Source theme's `navigation.hbs:38-46` and `post-list.hbs:101-115` emit `data-portal` buttons unconditionally when `@site.members_enabled` is truthy. With no Portal JS shipped, the buttons are silent no-ops — terrible first-run UX.

Fix: ship `dist/assets/nectar-portal.js` automatically (only when `@site.members_enabled = true` is configured) that:
  1. Locates every `[data-portal]` element
  2. For `signup` / `subscribe` opens the configured provider (Buttondown/Beehiiv embed or popup window)
  3. For `signin` / `account` either redirects to provider URL or no-ops with a console.warn
  4. For `upgrade` and `recommendations` shows a documented stub

Load via `{{ghost_foot}}` injection (analogous to how Ghost injects portal.min.js) so users don't have to touch the theme.

title: members: portal: no Buttondown adapter
body:
Buttondown is the most common Ghost alternative for indie newsletters. Document and implement a first-class adapter:
- `<form action="https://buttondown.email/api/emails/embed-subscribe/<user>">` for the inline form (`email-subscription.hbs` shape)
- Replace `data-members-form` with the Buttondown form action; convert `data-members-email` input name to `email`
- Optional: add hidden `tag` field from `@site.title` or post frontmatter

Keep the visual markup of `partials/email-subscription.hbs` intact — only the form's `action` and field names change at build time.

title: members: portal: no Beehiiv adapter
body:
Beehiiv exposes `https://embeds.beehiiv.com/<pub-id>?slim=true` as an iframe and `https://api.beehiiv.com/v2/publications/<pub-id>/subscriptions` as POST endpoint. Add adapter that:
- Rewrites `data-members-form` to POST to a Beehiiv-compatible form-handler URL (CORS-safe via Beehiiv's hosted endpoint)
- Replaces `data-portal="signup"` button clicks with opening the Beehiiv embed in a modal (or new window)
- Documents that paid tiers, account management, and member-only content gating are unsupported on the static side

title: members: portal: no Substack adapter
body: Substack publications expose `https://<pub>.substack.com/subscribe?just_signed_up=true` as a public signup form. Add adapter that rewrites `data-portal="signup"` to open this URL in a popup, and rewrites the embedded `<form data-members-form>` to POST to Substack's `https://substack.com/api/v1/free?nojs=true` (or use the official `subscribe-widget` iframe). Document the lack of per-domain branding control.

title: members: portal: no ConvertKit / Kit.com adapter
body: ConvertKit (now Kit.com) exposes `https://api.convertkit.com/v3/forms/<form-id>/subscribe` and a hosted form URL `https://<user>.ck.page/<form-id>`. Adapter rewrites `data-members-form` action and adds the `api_key` hidden field (or uses the hosted endpoint to avoid leaking the key). Document that magic-link signin is not supported.

title: members: portal: no Bento (bentonow) adapter
body: Bento is a popular Ghost-adjacent ESP. Their embed form posts to `https://app.bentonow.com/<site-uuid>/subscribe`. Add adapter parallel to Buttondown/Beehiiv.

title: members: portal: no MailerLite / Mailchimp / EmailOctopus adapters
body: Many migrators come from these providers. Provide a shared `[components.portal] provider = "mailerlite|mailchimp|emailoctopus"` with the relevant form action and field name remapping. Keep adapters in `src/components/portal/<provider>.ts` to make adding more straightforward.

title: members: portal: no `provider = "custom"` escape hatch with raw form action / field map
body:
Power users will want full control:
```toml
[components.portal]
provider = "custom"
form_action = "https://example.com/subscribe"
email_field_name = "EMAIL"
hidden_fields = { source = "nectar", list_id = "abc123" }
success_text = "Check your inbox to confirm"
```
Impl: the build-time transform reads these and rewrites `<form data-members-form>` with `method="POST"` and the relevant fields. Output success_text near `data-members-error`.

title: members: portal: no SSR-time removal mode (`provider = "none"`)
body:
When a user has no newsletter, they should be able to strip all signup UI from the build instead of leaving dead buttons. Add `[components.portal] provider = "none"` (or omit the section entirely) that strips elements matching `[data-portal]`, `[data-members-form]`, `[data-members-email]`, plus their wrapping `gh-footer-signup` / `gh-cta` ancestor sections.

Gate by a CSS-selector list configurable in `nectar.toml`.

title: members: context: `@member` undefined is correct but inconsistent — should be a proxy that swallows any property access
members: context: `@member.paid` / `@member.comped` / `@member.tier` need typed stubs
body:
Several Ghost themes (including Source's `post-list.hbs:104`) do `{{#unless @member.paid}}`. With `@member = undefined`, that resolves truthy (since `undefined.paid` → undefined → falsy in `unless`) — accidentally correct. But `{{@member.subscriptions.length}}` returns NaN strings in some themes.

Fix: document explicitly in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` that:
- `@member` is always undefined in static builds
- Nested access returns undefined (Handlebars-safe)
- `subscriptions`, `tiers`, `email`, `name`, `uuid`, `firstname`, `paid`, `comped`, `status` are all undefined

Add a render-time warning when a template reads `@member.*` so authors know they're hitting dead context.

title: members: context: `@site.members_enabled` should be derivable from `[components.portal].provider != "none"`
body:
Currently `@site.members_enabled` is not even set on SiteData (`src/content/model.ts:3-18`). Source theme branches on it 5× in `header.hbs`, `footer.hbs`, `cta.hbs`, `navigation.hbs`, `post-list.hbs`. Default is `undefined` → falsy → no signup UI ever renders.

Fix: derive from config: when `[components.portal]` is present with `provider != "none"`, set `site.members_enabled = true`. Add `members_enabled`, `paid_members_enabled`, `recommendations_enabled`, `members_invite_only` to SiteData with explicit defaults.

title: members: context: `@site.paid_members_enabled` and Stripe-keyed defaults
body: Ghost themes branch on `@site.paid_members_enabled` (Source: `post-list.hbs:103`) to decide whether to show 'Upgrade' button. In Nectar this should default `false` unless the user opts into a paid-tier-capable provider (Buttondown paid, Beehiiv premium, Substack paid). Add to SiteData + derive from provider config.

title: members: context: `@site.recommendations_enabled` flag and recommendations source
body: Source theme's `post-list.hbs:111` gates the sidebar recommendations block on `@site.recommendations_enabled`. Without it set, no recommendations appear (this happens to be the safe default — but the corresponding `{{recommendations}}` helper is unimplemented and would crash if reached). Add `site.recommendations_enabled` flag + a `[[recommendations]]` array in `nectar.toml` (see separate recommendations issues below).

title: members: context: `@site.members_invite_only` for invite-only publications
body: Source theme's `navigation.hbs:37` branches on `@site.members_invite_only` to hide the Subscribe button (only show Sign In). For Nectar, expose as `[components.portal] invite_only = true`. When set, the build-time DOM transform removes Subscribe buttons but keeps Sign In links.

title: members: tiers: no `{{#get "tiers"}}` data store
body:
Ghost exposes free / paid / comp tiers via `{{#get "tiers"}}` and `{{tiers}}`. Themes use them to render pricing tables. Nectar has zero tier concept. Decide policy:
- Option A: out of scope; document and make the helper return `[]` so iteration is no-op.
- Option B: declarative tiers in `nectar.toml`:
  ```toml
  [[tiers]]
  name = "Free"
  monthly_price = 0
  description = "Weekly newsletter"
  
  [[tiers]]
  name = "Premium"
  monthly_price = 9
  yearly_price = 90
  description = "Plus archives + Discord"
  signup_url = "https://buttondown.email/pay/premium"
  ```
  Then `{{#get "tiers"}}` resolves from this static list, `{{price}}` formats it, and Subscribe buttons route to per-tier signup URLs.

Recommendation: B, ranked P0 because tier-driven pricing pages are a top-3 reason migrators stay with Ghost.

title: members: tiers: no `{{price}}` helper
members: tiers: no `tier` context on member-visibility-keyed posts
body: Ghost posts can have `visibility = "tiers"` plus a `tiers: [premium]` list. Our Post type only knows `'public' | 'members' | 'paid'`. Extend to `visibility: 'public' | 'members' | 'paid' | 'tiers'` and add `tiers: string[]`. Visibility filter in `blocks.ts` already mostly handles 'paid'; extend.

title: members: tiers: no helper to render a pricing-table partial
body: Most Ghost themes ship a hand-written pricing-table that calls `{{#get "tiers" filter="type:paid"}}…`. We could ship a built-in partial `nectar/tiers-table.hbs` (registered automatically) so users can drop `{{> nectar/tiers-table}}` into a page. Make styling theme-scoped via classes (`gh-tiers`, `gh-tier-card`).

title: members: tiers: free-tier welcome page parity (`/welcome/free`)
body: Ghost auto-generates `/welcome/free/` and `/welcome/paid/` from the tier's `welcome_page_url`. Themes link to these from post-signup flows. For Nectar, accept `welcome_page_url` per `[[tiers]]` and emit those as actual content routes if the slug starts with `/welcome/`.

title: newsletter: no concept of email-only post vs publish+email post
body: Ghost posts have an `email_only` flag and an `email` object (subject, segment, replyTo). When `email_only=true` the post does not appear on the web — only goes out via email. We have no flag. Add `email_only: boolean` to Post frontmatter; when true, *do not* emit a public route. Emit instead a stub `/email-only/<slug>` only when `[build].emit_email_only_stub = true`.

title: newsletter: no separate email template (`email.hbs` / `email-template.hbs`)
body:
Ghost themes ship a parallel `email.hbs` for rendering newsletters (inlined CSS, table-based layout). Source theme doesn't have one but many do. Nectar should:
1. Detect `email.hbs` in the theme dir and skip rendering it as a web route
2. Optionally expose `nectar build:email --post=<slug>` that renders just the email template against a post and outputs MJML/inlined HTML to `dist/email/<slug>.html`
3. Document the workflow: pipe to Postmark/Resend/SES.

title: newsletter: email-only card markup (signup, email-cta, paywall) not handled in content pipeline
body:
Ghost's editor outputs `<div class="kg-card kg-signup-card">`, `<div class="kg-card kg-email-cta-card">`, `<div class="kg-card kg-paywall-card">` cards in post HTML. On the web, Ghost renders these as interactive components (signup card opens portal; paywall hides content below). For Nectar:
- `kg-signup-card`: replace with `partials/email-subscription.hbs` (or strip if portal disabled)
- `kg-email-cta-card`: strip entirely (it's email-only)
- `kg-paywall-card`: strip content below this card when visibility != public; keep when public
- `kg-button-card`: keep as-is (already plain link)

Implement as HTML post-processor in the content pipeline (`src/content/markdown.ts` adjacent).

title: newsletter: no Postmark/Resend/SES emit-on-publish hook
body: Real workflow for Ghost-leavers: write Markdown, build site, also email subscribers. Add `[components.newsletter]` provider with API key + list ID, plus `nectar publish:email --post=<slug>` command that posts the rendered HTML to the provider. Out of scope for first cut — issue is to track demand.

title: newsletter: `feature_image_caption` HTML stripped in email but kept on web — no policy doc
body: Per Ghost's docs, captions are HTML on web and plaintext-with-stripped-tags in email. Our render pipeline doesn't differentiate. Document the policy.

title: newsletter: post `excerpt` for email-list teaser truncation defaults differ between web and email
body: Ghost uses different excerpt lengths in newsletter (~140 chars) vs web (~250). Currently we use `custom_excerpt || excerpt || plaintext.slice(0, 250)` everywhere. Document; add `[build].email_excerpt_length` (default 140) if/when email emit is added.

title: recommendations: `{{recommendations}}` helper not implemented
body:
Source theme's `post-list.hbs:114` calls `{{recommendations}}`. We register no such helper — Handlebars throws `Missing helper: recommendations`. Already filed as P0 in self-audit but no concrete shape:

**Impl plan**:
1. Register `recommendations` helper that reads from `engine.content.recommendations`.
2. Add `[[recommendations]]` array to nectar.toml: `{ title, url, description, favicon, reason }` each.
3. Render via a built-in partial `nectar/recommendation-card.hbs` (or pass to theme via inverse block).
4. Honour `limit=` hash option (default 5).

Example:
```toml
[[recommendations]]
title = "Stratechery"
url = "https://stratechery.com"
description = "Ben Thompson on tech and strategy"
favicon = "/images/stratechery.png"
```

title: recommendations: no `data-portal="recommendations"` modal stub
body: Source's `post-list.hbs:115` has a 'See all' button with `data-portal="recommendations"`. Ghost opens a full-list modal with all recommendations. Nectar's portal shim should bind clicks on this selector to scroll to a `<section id="all-recommendations">` block that we auto-emit on a `/recommendations/` route when `[[recommendations]]` is configured.

title: recommendations: no incoming-recommendations directory listing
body: Ghost has a global recommendations directory at ghost.org/explore — sites can opt in. Not feasible for SSG (it's a network). Document as out-of-scope; suggest users link to https://ghost.org/explore/ if they want directory presence.

title: recommendations: favicon fetching at build time
body: If a `[[recommendations]]` entry has no `favicon`, the helper currently emits nothing. Add a build step that fetches `<recommendation.url>/favicon.ico` (or HEAD-resolves it from `<link rel=icon>`) once at build time, caches to `.nectar/favicons/`, and emits a hashed asset URL. Gate behind `[components.recommendations] fetch_favicons = true` (default false because it's network I/O).

title: access: `{{#unless access}}` evaluates to falsy in source theme's lock-icon path
body:
post-card.hbs:33 reads `{{#unless access}}{{^has visibility="public"}}{{> "icons/lock"}}{{/has}}{{/unless}}`. `access` is undefined → `unless access` is truthy → we enter the block → `{{^has visibility="public"}}` (i.e. `unless visibility==='public'`) → if the post is non-public, render lock icon.

Problem: every post for non-logged-in users *should* be locked (because access is always denied in static-build land). Today our example renders lock icons on member/paid posts (good). But there's no helper named `access` registered, so Handlebars actually silently treats it as `undefined` (no helper warning because it could be a context property). Cleaner: register an `access` helper that returns false in builds, and document that themes can rely on this contract.

title: access: no policy for member/paid posts at build time
body:
Posts with `visibility: members | paid` currently render their full HTML to the public route (we have no auth). For Ghost parity:
- Default: render the first N words (configurable, default ~300) and append `<div class="gh-paywall-stub">` with a 'Subscribe to read more' CTA wired to portal
- Optional `[content].visibility_policy = "render-full" | "truncate" | "skip"` controlling whether we leak the full content, truncate, or skip the route entirely
- For `truncate`, use the same truncation as Ghost's paywall (`<!-- members -->` HTML comment marker; otherwise word count)

title: access: `<!-- members-only -->` marker in Markdown for paywall point
body: Ghost editor inserts a paywall card that splits the post HTML. For Markdown-authored content, support a magic line `<!-- members-only -->` that becomes the paywall split point. Above the line → public preview; below → gated. Render an interpolated CTA in place.

title: access: post.access boolean not exposed in post context
body: Add `access: false` to Post object exposed via context (always false in builds; corresponds to logged-out viewer). Themes' `{{#if access}}` would evaluate falsy; `{{#unless access}}` truthy. Matches Ghost's behavior for anonymous viewers.

title: access: paywall CTA partial not provided
body:
When we truncate a members-only post, we need to insert SOMETHING in place of the cut content. Provide a built-in partial `nectar/paywall.hbs` themes can override: ```hbs

{{t "This post is for members only"}}

``` Allow override by placing `partials/paywall.hbs` in theme.

title: subscribe-forms: `<form data-members-form>` posts to nonexistent `/members/api/send-magic-link`
body:
Source's `email-subscription.hbs` is `<form class="gh-form" data-members-form>` with no `action` attribute. Ghost's runtime JS binds to the `data-members-form` selector and POSTs to `/members/api/send-magic-link`. In Nectar build with no portal provider, this form will submit to the current URL — bad UX.

Fix (paired with the portal adapter): the build-time DOM transform sets `<form>.action` to the provider's endpoint and adjusts the input `name` if needed (e.g. Buttondown uses `email`; Mailchimp uses `EMAIL`).

title: subscribe-forms: `<input data-members-email>` field name not provider-aware
body: Same issue: the input is `<input … data-members-email>` without `name="email"`. Buttondown/Beehiiv expect `name="email"`; Mailchimp expects `name="EMAIL"`. Build-time transform must add the correct name attribute.

title: subscribe-forms: no honeypot / spam protection in default form rewrite
body: When we rewrite `data-members-form` to a provider URL, also inject a hidden honeypot field (`<input name="website" tabindex="-1" autocomplete="off" style="display:none">`). Buttondown / Beehiiv ignore it; bots fill it; we can reject server-side. Document for `provider = "custom"` users.

title: subscribe-forms: no client-side success/error UX without provider JS
body:
Source's form has `

` and a `Email sent` for success. Ghost's portal JS toggles these. With our shim, after the form POSTs to provider, the user is taken to the provider's success page — `data-members-error` and the success span are dead. Fix: include a tiny ~1KB inline JS that does fetch(form.action, …) and toggles success/error classes locally. Optional; off by default; opt-in via `[components.portal] inline_submit = true`.

title: subscribe-forms: `{{subscribe_form}}` helper renders a useless placeholder
body: content.ts:116 renders `<form data-nectar-subscribe action="#" method="post"><input type="email" name="email" /></form>`. Action is `#`. Real themes use this helper from custom layouts. Wire it to the same provider-rewrite pipeline as `data-members-form`.

title: subscribe-forms: `{{input_email}}` helper emits `data-members-email` but no `name=`
body: content.ts:122 outputs `<input data-members-email type="email" required placeholder="…">`. Missing `name="email"`. Without it, a fallback browser submit posts no payload. Add `name="email"` by default; let the provider adapter override.

title: search: `data-ghost-search` button has no runtime
body:
Source theme's `search-toggle.hbs` and `header-content.hbs:75` use `data-ghost-search`. Ghost ships sodo-search (Ember-based modal). Nectar has no search runtime — clicks do nothing. Already filed as `search: no Pagefind integration` (P1) but doesn't address the `data-ghost-search` selector specifically.

Fix: when `[components.search]` is configured, automatically inject a tiny JS shim that:
1. Locates `[data-ghost-search]` triggers
2. Opens a modal with Pagefind/Algolia/Meilisearch UI
3. Wires keyboard shortcut (cmd+K / ctrl+K)

title: search: no Algolia / Meilisearch adapter scaffolds
body:
Beyond Pagefind, document two more common providers:
- Algolia: emit a `dist/.nectar/algolia-records.json` that users push via `algoliasearch` CLI; provide DocSearch-compatible CSS classes.
- Meilisearch: emit the same JSON shape; provide a `nectar push:meilisearch` thin wrapper around `meilisearch-js`.
Keep emit-only logic in nectar; pushing is user's responsibility.

title: search: index excludes `visibility != public` posts by default
body: Member/paid posts should not leak into client-side search indexes (Pagefind indexes everything in dist/). Add `<meta name="pagefind-skip">` automatically on routes whose source post has non-public visibility; document Algolia/Meili equivalents.

title: search: `gh-search` partial in Source theme leaks CSS classes that suggest portal-bound UI
body: Document that the Source theme's `.gh-search` button is a *trigger* — Nectar's responsibility is to bind it. Otherwise theme authors blame the theme for a non-functional button.

title: comments: `{{comments}}` helper renders an empty `<div data-nectar-comments>` with no provider wiring
body:
content.ts:112 emits `
`. Themes call `{{#if comments}}{{comments}}{{/if}}` (Source: post.hbs:60). Without a provider, the div is invisible. Fix: add `[components.comments] provider = "giscus|disqus|utterances|cusdis|hyvor|isso"` and have the helper emit the right script tag inline. Already partially filed (`comments: no Giscus / Disqus / Utterances wiring` P1) but specifically mention `data-nectar-comments` selector contract so users have a single place to override.

title: comments: post.comments boolean wired from frontmatter but never read by `{{comments}}` helper
body: loader.ts:349 sets `comments: true` unconditionally. Post type has the field. But the `comments` helper ignores it; it always emits the div. Honour the per-post flag: when `false`, render nothing.

title: webhooks: no concept of build-completion or content-change webhook
body: Ghost admin sends webhooks on post.published etc. Nectar is a build tool — analogue is a post-build hook. Add `[hooks] post_build = "./scripts/notify-discord.sh"` (already covered partly by `cli: no build hooks` in self-audit?). Specifically for member-facing flow, recommend invoking the newsletter-send command from this hook.

title: webhooks: integration directory not modelled
body: Ghost has a `/ghost/api/integrations` admin concept (Zapier, Slack, etc.). Out of scope; document.

title: activitypub: experimental Ghost Fediverse plugin not modelled
body: Ghost 5.x added experimental ActivityPub support (one-account-per-publication). For Nectar, recommend external tools (Bridgy Fed, fed.brid.gy) since static sites can't actually respond to AP signed requests. Document as out-of-scope with link to user guidance.

title: activitypub: WebFinger / .well-known endpoints from Ghost not emitted
body: Ghost emits `/.well-known/webfinger` for ActivityPub. We don't. Add optional emit of static WebFinger response from `[components.activitypub] handle = "@me@example.com"` config — purely static (no incoming request handling). Lets users get found by their fediverse handle.

title: analytics: no built-in member analytics dashboard
body: Ghost ships a `/ghost/#/dashboard` with member growth, MRR, open rates. Static sites can't surface live data. Document that users should rely on their ESP's own analytics (Buttondown / Beehiiv / Substack dashboards) — link from `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`.

title: analytics: no privacy-friendly view tracking out of the box
body: Many Ghost users use Plausible/Umami/Fathom alongside. Add `[components.analytics] provider = "plausible|umami|fathom|simpleanalytics|googleanalytics" site = "…"` that injects the right snippet into `{{ghost_head}}` automatically. Honour DNT and respect IP-anonymisation flags.

title: analytics: no membership-tier-level segmentation source
body: Member-segmented analytics (free vs paid open rates) live in Ghost's email send logs. Document gap: refer users to their ESP's analytics; in Nectar, no per-tier data crosses the static boundary.

title: members: end-to-end docs page — `docs/MEMBERS.md` does not exist
body:
Create a dedicated guide (linked from README and GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md) walking through:
1. The migration path from Ghost members → Buttondown / Beehiiv / Substack
2. What `@member`, `@site.members_enabled`, `{{#unless access}}` mean in Nectar
3. How the portal adapter rewrites buttons and forms
4. Examples for each supported provider
5. Known parity gaps (no per-user state, no tier checks, no signin / account / upgrade in the static page itself)

P0 because new users have no orientation without it.

title: members: example theme should keep portal markup working in default `nectar build`
body: Right now `example/themes/source` is shipped with portal markup but no portal provider config in `example/nectar.toml`. Running `nectar build` produces a site with dead `data-portal` buttons — a bad showcase. Add a sensible `[components.portal] provider = "none"` (so subscribe UI is stripped) OR `provider = "buttondown"` with a placeholder publication for the example.

title: members: i18n: portal strings ("Subscribe", "Sign in", "Account", "Email sent") all hard-coded in theme
body: Source theme wraps every portal-related string in `{{t "Subscribe"}}` etc. Verify our `t` helper resolves them from theme `locales/<lang>.json`. Add to the locale test fixture: ensure `"Subscribe"`, `"Sign in"`, `"Account"`, `"Email sent"`, `"jamie@example.com"`, `"Recommendations"`, `"See all"`, `"Upgrade"`, `"Search this site"`, `"Search posts, tags and authors"`, `"This post is for members only"` all translate.

title: members: error reporting — silent no-op when portal config is malformed
body: If `[components.portal] provider = "buttondown"` is set but `publication` is missing, the build currently passes (because we don't validate). Make portal config a zod sub-schema with `superRefine` requiring provider-specific fields. Fail the build with a precise message.

title: members: build artefact — emit `dist/.nectar/portal-manifest.json` listing every rewritten selector
body: For debugging: when the portal adapter rewrites `[data-portal]` / `[data-members-form]` / `[data-ghost-search]` elements, record per-file counts in `dist/.nectar/portal-manifest.json`. Surface a summary in build output (`portal: rewrote 18 elements across 12 routes`).

title: members: theme-package.json contract — themes may declare `members: required` in their package.json
body: Ghost themes' `package.json` has `"config": { "members": "required" }` (some themes refuse to install if members feature is disabled). Decide policy: warn at theme-load time if a theme declares `members: required` but no portal provider is configured.

title: members: `{{#contentFor}}` block helper missing — used by many member-facing themes to swap default partials
body: Ghost themes can declare `{{#contentFor "some-block"}}…{{/contentFor}}` in a child template and have it injected into a `{{{block "some-block"}}}` slot in the layout. Not used by Source but common in member-portal themes. Implement to unblock more migrations.

title: members: `cancel_link` / `signup_url` helpers missing
body: Some Ghost themes use `{{cancel_link}}` (for paid members) and `{{signup_url}}`. Already partly noted in self-audit's missing-helpers issue but worth its own stub: `cancel_link` → undefined / empty, `signup_url` → portal provider's signup URL when configured.

title: members: `member_count` / `comment_count` helpers absent
body: `{{member_count}}` is used in social-proof phrasing ("Join 1,234 readers"). Static builds can't know live counts. Stub returns empty string; allow override via `[components.portal] member_count = 1234` (manual). Document.

title: members: gscan-compatible lint output for member-related theme fields
body: Ghost's `gscan` validator flags missing portal markup. If we add a `nectar theme lint` command (separate issue), include rules for: themes that read `@member.*` should warn that this is a static build; themes that include `data-portal` triggers without portal config should warn; themes that reference `{{recommendations}}` without `[[recommendations]]` should warn.
